title: Calculator Page Layout
requirement: When the user opens the Calculator page, UI-ui.layout shall display a two-column layout with the input card on the left, the results panel on the right, and the Calculate button at the bottom-right of the input card.
acceptance_criteria: When the page loads, the input card and results panel are visible in the specified positions and the Calculate button is rendered at the bottom-right of the input card.

title: Input Field Arrangement
requirement: When the user views the input card, UI-ui.input_fields shall render fields in this top-to-bottom order: sex, age, height_cm, weight_kg, activity_level, goal, and macro_preset, with labels and helper text beside each field.
acceptance_criteria: When the input card is rendered, all seven fields appear in the defined order with visible labels and helper text.

title: Macro Preset Guidance
requirement: When the user selects a macro preset option, UI-ui.preset_help shall update a preset guidance panel below the selector to show target ratio and plain-language description for the selected preset.
acceptance_criteria: When the user changes preset from Balanced to High Protein or Low Carb, the guidance panel updates immediately with the selected preset ratio and description.

title: Pre-Submit Confirmation Dialog
requirement: When the user clicks the Calculate button with a valid form, UI-ui.submit_dialog shall open a confirmation dialog centered on the page and display a summary of entered profile values with Confirm and Cancel actions.
acceptance_criteria: When the user clicks Calculate on a valid form, the confirmation dialog opens with profile summary lines and both Confirm and Cancel actions.

title: Submit Request Workflow
requirement: When the user clicks Confirm in the confirmation dialog, UI-ui.submit_flow shall send one POST request to `/api/v1/nutrition/calculate`, close the dialog, and show an in-card loading state until a response is received.
acceptance_criteria: When Confirm is clicked once, one POST request is sent, the dialog closes, and loading state is visible until the request completes.

title: Success Result Rendering
requirement: When the user receives an HTTP 200 response, UI-ui.success_render shall populate the results panel with calories_per_day, protein_g, carbs_g, fat_g, and a calculation timestamp block.
acceptance_criteria: When a 200 response arrives, the results panel shows all five values using response data and the timestamp block becomes visible.

title: Validation Error Feedback
requirement: When the user receives an HTTP 422 response, UI-ui.validation_feedback shall map each server field error to its matching input field and display an error summary banner at the top of the input card.
acceptance_criteria: When a 422 response contains field errors, matching input fields display error text and the summary banner lists the same fields.

title: Rate Limit Feedback
requirement: When the user receives an HTTP 429 response, UI-ui.rate_limit_feedback shall show a cooldown banner above the form with retry_seconds and enable a Retry action when the countdown reaches zero.
acceptance_criteria: When a 429 response includes retry_seconds, the banner countdown matches retry_seconds and the Retry action becomes enabled at countdown completion.

title: Server Error Recovery UX
requirement: When the user receives an HTTP 500 response, UI-ui.server_error_feedback shall open an error modal containing a request_id message, keep current form values visible, and provide Retry and Close actions.
acceptance_criteria: When a 500 response arrives, an error modal shows request_id, form values remain present, and Retry and Close actions are available.

title: Dependency Registry Handshake
requirement: When the system starts the FastAPI application, API-api.startup_registry shall run a startup handshake that verifies CORE calculator service, DATA food provider adapter, and OBS metrics recorder are registered and callable.
acceptance_criteria: When application startup completes, handshake status for CORE, DATA, and OBS is recorded as ready or degraded in startup state.

title: Live Health Endpoint
requirement: When the user sends GET `/health/live`, API-api.health_live shall return HTTP 200 with a payload containing service_name, service_version, and current_time_utc.
acceptance_criteria: When `/health/live` is called, response status is 200 and payload contains service_name, service_version, and current_time_utc fields.

title: Readiness Health Endpoint
requirement: When the user sends GET `/health/ready`, API-api.health_ready shall evaluate startup handshake state and return HTTP 200 for ready state or HTTP 503 for degraded state with component-level readiness details.
acceptance_criteria: When startup state is ready, `/health/ready` returns 200 with component details. When startup state is degraded, `/health/ready` returns 503 with component details.

title: Request Parsing Entry
requirement: When the user sends POST `/api/v1/nutrition/calculate`, API-api.request_parse shall parse the JSON body into the shared request DTO and attach request_id to request context before business processing.
acceptance_criteria: When a POST request is received, parsed DTO and request_id are both available in endpoint context.

title: Boundary Normalization
requirement: When the system receives a parsed request DTO, API-api.request_normalize shall normalize units and enumerations into canonical domain values before invoking CORE calculation logic.
acceptance_criteria: When a parsed request enters normalization, the output payload uses canonical enum names and normalized numeric units.

title: Calculation Orchestration
requirement: When the system completes request normalization, API-api.compute_orchestration shall invoke CORE calorie and macro calculation service and return shared response DTO fields in the API response body.
acceptance_criteria: When CORE service returns successfully, API response includes calories_per_day, protein_g, carbs_g, fat_g, and goal_adjustment fields.

title: Domain Validation Mapping
requirement: When the system receives a domain validation exception from CORE, API-api.domain_error_map shall return HTTP 422 with field_errors and a stable error_code for each invalid field.
acceptance_criteria: When CORE raises validation exception, API responds with status 422 and structured field_errors plus stable error_code values.

title: Provider Failure Mapping
requirement: When the system receives a provider unavailable exception from DATA, API-api.provider_error_map shall return HTTP 503 with retry guidance and include request_id in the response body.
acceptance_criteria: When DATA raises provider unavailable exception, API responds with status 503 and payload includes retry guidance plus request_id.

title: Rate Limit Middleware
requirement: When the user sends requests above the configured per-IP threshold, API-api.rate_limit_middleware shall return HTTP 429 with retry_seconds and emit a rate_limit metric event.
acceptance_criteria: When request volume exceeds threshold for an IP, response status is 429 with retry_seconds and one rate_limit metric event is emitted.

title: Request ID Middleware
requirement: When the user sends any API request, API-api.request_id_middleware shall generate or accept a request_id, store it in request context, and include it in every response header.
acceptance_criteria: For each API request, request context contains request_id and response header includes the same request_id value.

title: Response Envelope Shape
requirement: When the system returns a successful nutrition response, API-api.response_shape shall include result fields and metadata fields request_id, calculated_at_utc, and calculation_version in the response envelope.
acceptance_criteria: When a successful response is produced, payload contains result fields and metadata fields request_id, calculated_at_utc, and calculation_version.

title: Domain Profile Validation
requirement: When the system receives a nutrition profile from API, CORE-core.profile_validation shall validate age, height_cm, weight_kg, sex, activity_level, and goal against configured domain ranges.
acceptance_criteria: When profile values are outside configured ranges, validation returns structured domain validation errors with field names and messages.

title: BMR Formula Calculation
requirement: When the system receives a validated profile, CORE-core.bmr_formula shall calculate basal metabolic rate using the Mifflin-St Jeor formula for the profile sex and body measurements.
acceptance_criteria: When validated profile inputs are provided, calculated BMR matches reference vectors for male and female cases within configured tolerance.

title: TDEE Formula Calculation
requirement: When the system has a computed BMR and activity level, CORE-core.tdee_formula shall compute TDEE by applying the configured activity multiplier to BMR.
acceptance_criteria: When BMR and activity level are provided, TDEE equals BMR multiplied by the configured activity multiplier for that activity level.

title: Goal Calorie Adjustment
requirement: When the system has computed TDEE and user goal, CORE-core.goal_adjustment shall apply goal policy to derive target daily calories and include applied policy label in result metadata.
acceptance_criteria: When goal is maintain, lose, or gain, target calories and policy label are returned using configured adjustment parameters.

title: Macro Allocation Policy
requirement: When the system has target daily calories and macro preset, CORE-core.macro_allocation shall allocate calories across protein, carbs, and fat according to preset ratios before gram conversion.
acceptance_criteria: When preset is Balanced, High Protein, or Low Carb, macro calorie shares match configured ratio for that preset.

title: Rounding and Reconciliation
requirement: When the system converts macro calories to grams, CORE-core.rounding_reconcile shall round macro grams to configured precision and reconcile totals to stay within configured calorie tolerance.
acceptance_criteria: When macro grams are rounded, reconstructed calories remain within configured tolerance of target daily calories.

title: Nutrition Request Contract
requirement: When the system serializes or deserializes a nutrition calculation request, SHARED-shared.request_contract shall enforce a canonical DTO with explicit field names, field types, and enum sets used by UI and API.
acceptance_criteria: When request DTO is used in UI or API, field names and enum values match the canonical shared contract definition.

title: Nutrition Response Contract
requirement: When the system serializes or deserializes a nutrition calculation response, SHARED-shared.response_contract shall enforce a canonical DTO containing result values and metadata fields used by API and UI.
acceptance_criteria: When response DTO is produced by API and consumed by UI, result and metadata fields match the shared contract definition.

title: External Provider Handshake
requirement: When the system starts the DATA adapter, DATA-data.provider_handshake shall verify provider credentials and connectivity by running a startup handshake request and recording handshake status.
acceptance_criteria: When DATA startup runs, provider handshake status is recorded as connected or degraded with a timestamp.

title: Food Search Workflow
requirement: When the system receives a normalized food search query, DATA-data.search_workflow shall call the external provider search API and map provider records into internal food item DTO fields.
acceptance_criteria: When a normalized query is submitted, mapped food item DTO records are returned with internal field names and units.

title: Provider Retry and Timeout
requirement: When the system receives timeout or transient provider errors during food search, DATA-data.retry_timeout shall execute configured retry attempts with backoff and return provider status after final attempt.
acceptance_criteria: When transient errors occur, retry attempts follow configured count and backoff schedule, and final status is returned after last attempt.

title: Food Search Cache Flow
requirement: When the system processes a normalized search query, DATA-data.cache_flow shall read cache by normalized key and return cached response on hit or store fresh mapped response on miss.
acceptance_criteria: When cache hit occurs, cached response is returned with cache_hit flag. When cache miss occurs, fresh response is stored and returned with cache_hit flag set to false.

title: Request Metrics Recording
requirement: When the system completes an API request, OBS-obs.metrics_record shall record a metrics event containing endpoint, status_code, duration_ms, and request_id.
acceptance_criteria: When any API request completes, one metrics event is persisted with endpoint, status_code, duration_ms, and request_id.

title: Structured Log Recording
requirement: When the system emits an application log for request processing, OBS-obs.log_record shall write a structured log entry containing timestamp_utc, request_id, event_name, and key workflow attributes.
acceptance_criteria: When request workflow logs are emitted, each entry includes timestamp_utc, request_id, event_name, and workflow attributes in structured format.

title: Login Page Layout
requirement: When the user opens the Sign In page, UI-ui.login_layout shall display email and password fields in a centered auth card with a Sign In button below the fields and a Remember Me checkbox under the password field.
acceptance_criteria: When the Sign In page loads, the auth card shows email field, password field, Remember Me checkbox, and Sign In button in the defined order.

title: Login Request Trigger
requirement: When the user clicks the Sign In button with valid credentials entered, UI-ui.login_request_send shall send one POST request to `/api/v1/auth/login` containing email, password, and remember_me values.
acceptance_criteria: When Sign In is clicked with valid field values, one POST request is sent with email, password, and remember_me fields.

title: Login Request DTO Contract
requirement: When the system serializes or deserializes an authentication request, SHARED-shared.login_request_contract shall enforce a canonical LoginRequest DTO with email, password, and remember_me fields.
acceptance_criteria: When LoginRequest is used by UI and API, field names and types match the shared contract definition.

title: Login Response DTO Contract
requirement: When the system serializes or deserializes an authentication response, SHARED-shared.login_response_contract shall enforce a canonical LoginResponse DTO containing access_token, refresh_token, expires_at_utc, and user_profile fields.
acceptance_criteria: When LoginResponse is produced by API and consumed by UI, access_token, refresh_token, expires_at_utc, and user_profile fields match the shared contract.

title: Login Request Parsing
requirement: When the user sends POST `/api/v1/auth/login`, API-api.login_request_parse shall parse the JSON body into LoginRequest DTO and attach request_id before credential verification workflow starts.
acceptance_criteria: When POST `/api/v1/auth/login` is received, LoginRequest DTO and request_id are available in request context.

title: Credential Lookup Workflow
requirement: When the system receives a login lookup call from API, DATA-data.credential_lookup shall fetch user credential record by normalized email and return password_hash, hash_algorithm, and account_status.
acceptance_criteria: When API requests credential lookup for an email, DATA returns password_hash, hash_algorithm, and account_status for matching user.

title: Password Verification Policy
requirement: When the system receives plaintext password and stored hash data, CORE-core.password_verify shall verify the password using configured hash algorithm and return verification_result and failure_reason.
acceptance_criteria: When valid password and hash pair are provided, verification_result is success. When invalid pair is provided, verification_result is failed with failure_reason.

title: Token Issuance Workflow
requirement: When the system receives successful credential verification, API-api.login_token_issue shall issue access_token and refresh_token with expiry values based on remember_me policy.
acceptance_criteria: When verification_result is success, API generates access_token, refresh_token, and expiry values matching remember_me policy windows.

title: Login Success Response
requirement: When the system completes token issuance, API-api.login_response_return shall return HTTP 200 with LoginResponse payload including user_profile and token expiry metadata.
acceptance_criteria: When login succeeds, HTTP status is 200 and response body contains LoginResponse fields from shared contract.

title: Login Success Navigation
requirement: When the user receives HTTP 200 from login, UI-ui.login_success_transition shall store session tokens in configured client storage and navigate the user to the Calculator page.
acceptance_criteria: When login response status is 200, tokens are stored and navigation lands on Calculator page.

title: Login Error Feedback
requirement: When the user receives HTTP 401 from login, UI-ui.login_error_feedback shall show an inline auth error banner above the email field and keep entered email value visible for retry.
acceptance_criteria: When login response status is 401, auth error banner appears and email field retains the submitted value.

title: Session Validation Endpoint
requirement: When the user sends GET `/api/v1/auth/session` with bearer token, API-api.session_validate shall validate token signature and expiry and return active session profile for valid token.
acceptance_criteria: When token is valid, `/api/v1/auth/session` returns HTTP 200 with session profile. When token is expired or invalid, endpoint returns HTTP 401 with error code.

title: Session Restore On Startup
requirement: When the user opens the application with stored tokens, UI-ui.session_restore shall call `/api/v1/auth/session` and restore authenticated UI state after successful validation.
acceptance_criteria: When stored token is valid, authenticated navigation and user header state are restored after session validation call.

title: History Page Layout
requirement: When the user opens the History page, UI-ui.history_layout shall display a filter bar at the top, a paginated result table in the center, and a detail drawer on the right for selected history records.
acceptance_criteria: When the History page loads, filter bar, paginated table, and detail drawer are visible in the specified layout.

title: History Query Request Trigger
requirement: When the user clicks Apply Filters on the History page, UI-ui.history_query_send shall send one GET request to `/api/v1/history` with date_range, goal, and pagination query parameters.
acceptance_criteria: When Apply Filters is clicked, one GET request is sent with date_range, goal, and pagination parameters matching UI filter values.

title: History Query Handling
requirement: When the user sends GET `/api/v1/history`, API-api.history_query_handle shall validate filter and pagination parameters, authorize user access scope, and invoke DATA history read workflow.
acceptance_criteria: When history query is received, validated parameters and authorized user scope are passed to DATA read workflow.

title: History Retention Write Workflow
requirement: When the system returns a successful nutrition calculation response, DATA-data.history_write shall persist a history record containing user_id, input_profile_snapshot, result_snapshot, and created_at_utc.
acceptance_criteria: When calculation completes successfully, one history record is stored with user_id, input_profile_snapshot, result_snapshot, and created_at_utc.

title: History Retention Read Workflow
requirement: When the system receives an authorized history query, DATA-data.history_read shall return records filtered by user_id and query filters sorted by created_at_utc descending.
acceptance_criteria: When history query is executed, returned records match user_id scope and are sorted newest to oldest.

title: History Response Envelope
requirement: When the system returns history data to UI, API-api.history_response_shape shall include items, total_count, page, page_size, and has_next_page fields in the response envelope.
acceptance_criteria: When history response is produced, envelope contains items, total_count, page, page_size, and has_next_page fields.

title: History Item Restore Action
requirement: When the user clicks Restore on a history row, UI-ui.history_restore_action shall populate the Calculator form with that record input_profile_snapshot and open the confirmation dialog before recalculation.
acceptance_criteria: When Restore is clicked for a history row, Calculator form fields match selected input snapshot and confirmation dialog becomes visible.

title: Export Dialog Workflow
requirement: When the user clicks Export on the History page, UI-ui.export_dialog shall open a modal containing format selector, date range selector, and Include Input Details checkbox.
acceptance_criteria: When Export is clicked, export modal appears with format selector, date range selector, and Include Input Details checkbox.

title: Export Request Trigger
requirement: When the user clicks Generate Export in the modal, UI-ui.export_request_send shall send one POST request to `/api/v1/history/export` containing export_format, date_range, and include_input_details fields.
acceptance_criteria: When Generate Export is clicked, one POST request is sent with export_format, date_range, and include_input_details values from modal controls.

title: Export Request DTO Contract
requirement: When the system serializes or deserializes an export request, SHARED-shared.export_request_contract shall enforce a canonical ExportRequest DTO with export_format, date_range_start, date_range_end, and include_input_details fields.
acceptance_criteria: When ExportRequest is used by UI and API, field names and types match the shared contract.

title: Export Request Handling
requirement: When the user sends POST `/api/v1/history/export`, API-api.export_request_handle shall validate export parameters, authorize user scope, and invoke CORE export payload build workflow.
acceptance_criteria: When export request is received, validated parameters and authorized scope are passed to CORE export payload build workflow.

title: Export Payload Build
requirement: When the system receives validated export parameters, CORE-core.export_payload_build shall transform history records into ordered export rows with selected columns and locale-specific date formatting rules.
acceptance_criteria: When export payload is built, rows are ordered by created_at_utc descending and selected columns match requested export settings.

title: Export Artifact Persistence
requirement: When the system receives generated export content, DATA-data.export_artifact_store shall store the artifact with user ownership metadata and return artifact_id, file_name, and expiration_utc.
acceptance_criteria: When export artifact is persisted, DATA returns artifact_id, file_name, and expiration_utc fields for the stored artifact.

title: Export Link Response
requirement: When the system receives artifact metadata from DATA, API-api.export_response_link shall return HTTP 200 with artifact_id, download_url, and expiration_utc in the response payload.
acceptance_criteria: When export creation succeeds, HTTP 200 response includes artifact_id, download_url, and expiration_utc fields.

title: Export Download Flow
requirement: When the user receives successful export response, UI-ui.export_download_flow shall show an export ready banner with Download File action and open the download_url in a new browser tab when clicked.
acceptance_criteria: When export response is successful, export ready banner appears and Download File action opens the returned download_url.

title: Auth History Export Metrics
requirement: When the system completes login, history query, or export generation workflows, OBS-obs.auth_history_export_metrics shall record metrics events with request_id, workflow_type, status_code, and duration_ms.
acceptance_criteria: When login, history, or export workflow completes, one metrics event is recorded with request_id, workflow_type, status_code, and duration_ms.
